section.title: Chief Complaint
section.text: Patient presents with chest pain radiating to the left arm with onset approximately two hours prior to arrival. Patient reports associated shortness of breath and diaphoresis. No prior cardiac history documented in the medical record.

section.title: History of Present Illness
section.text: A 58-year-old male with hypertension and type 2 diabetes mellitus presents to the emergency department with acute onset chest pain. The pain is described as pressure-like, rated 8 out of 10 in severity. ECG on arrival shows ST elevation in leads II, III, and aVF consistent with inferior myocardial infarction.
